source: Ash feature request 2026-06-21 â€” output should be smart, easy to understand, cleanly formatted, extremely well written, in BOTH modes.
title: Research: clean, well-written, cited output contract (normie + root)
current_state: Raw LLM text is returned untouched (pi_agent final_text = resp.text). Neither root nor normie prompt gives any guidance on formatting research answers â€” no structure, no 'cite your sources', no readability standard. Output quality is whatever the model defaults to.
target_state: A shared, explicit OUTPUT CONTRACT for research/informational answers, applied in BOTH modes (scaled to each): lead with the direct answer, then tight scannable structure (short paragraphs / bullets / a small table when it helps), distinguish fact from interpretation, and end with a 'Sources' list when tools were used (titles + URLs from grounded_search/deep_research). Normie = concise version of the same contract; root = fuller. The contract lives in the prompts; a light post-step ensures a Sources section is present when citations exist.
migration_plan: 1. Author a concise OUTPUT CONTRACT block (answer-first; scannable structure; fact vs interpretation; Sources section when tools used; no walls of text). Add it to prompts/consciousness.txt (root, fuller) and prompts/consciousness_normie.txt (normie, terse) â€” keep wording consistent so behavior matches across modes.
2. Ensure the contract only triggers for substantive/informational answers (don't bureaucratize a one-line chat reply) â€” phrase it as 'when answering a question that used research/tools'.

3. Light post-step in the response path: if the turn used grounded_search/deep_research and the model omitted a Sources section, append one built from the tool citations. Keep it minimal and idempotent (no double Sources).
4. Verify against the T-192 consciousness tool-drift check so prompt edits don't trip verify.py's drift gate.

5. Tests: testing/test_output_contract.py â€” both prompts contain the contract; the post-step appends Sources when citations exist and the model omitted them; it does NOT duplicate an existing Sources section; non-research chat replies are unaffected.
risk_notes: Most of this is prompt-craft (cheap, high-impact); keep the code post-step tiny and idempotent. Watch the T-192 tool-drift gate and the T-168 ABOUT.md/consciousness checks in verify.py when editing prompts. Don't over-format casual chat â€” the contract is for research/informational answers only.
